hooks.prompt: Valleys at Ashebrook docs freshness check — detection only; you must NOT edit any file. Step 1: run exactly `git status --porcelain` as a single command (it is pre-approved; do not add pipes, redirects, `||`, or chain it with anything — compound commands are not covered by the permission rules and will be denied). If you need content detail, `git diff HEAD` and `git diff HEAD --name-only` are also pre-approved as standalone commands. Step 2: if no changed or untracked files are under src/, scripts/, or test/, and neither wrangler.toml, astro.config.mjs, nor package.json changed, allow the stop — respond that no source files changed. Step 3: otherwise use the Read, Grep, and Glob tools (no other shell commands) to spot-check whether the docs owned by .claude/agents/docs-updater.md — AGENTS.md (endpoints, data model, commands, bindings), README.md, SETUP.md, SECURITY.md, and CHANGELOG.md — still match the changed code. Check only sections relevant to the changed files, not a full audit. Step 4: if the docs are consistent, allow the stop. If there is drift, block the stop with a reason that lists the changed source files, the specific stale doc sections, and this instruction: 'Invoke the docs-updater subagent to fix exactly this drift, then finish.'
